transition_style: Seamless, continuous FPV-style flythroughs, utilizing frame-matched doorway passes for smooth transitions between interior rooms. Hard cuts are reserved for major scene changes, such as transitioning from exterior to interior, or between distinct floors/areas when a continuous path is not feasible.
color_grade: Bright, natural, and clean with good contrast, avoiding oversaturation.
global_do: Maintain continuous, smooth, and flowing FPV-style camera movements, simulating a single drone take.
Utilize forward/backward dollies, lateral trucks, and shallow orbits to showcase rooms from multiple angles, prioritizing lateral parallax.
Employ frame-matched doorway passes for seamless transitions between interior rooms.
Vary camera height slightly to emphasize architectural features (e.g., grand staircase) and maintain a natural, eye-level perspective in interiors.
Keep subjects centered or in the rule-of-thirds during pans and orbits.
Ensure good, even lighting, highlighting natural light and avoiding harsh shadows or overexposure.
Showcase key architectural features, large windows, and unique structures with deliberate camera moves.
Include establishing and concluding aerial drone shots for exterior context and a comprehensive overview.
Maintain a consistent, bright, natural, and clean color grade with good contrast.
Ensure constant speed, steady gimbal, and no rotation or warping.
global_dont: Avoid hard cuts or abrupt transitions within continuous flythrough segments; prioritize seamless, frame-matched doorway passes.
Do not use shaky, jerky, erratic, or unstable camera movements.
Avoid overly fast or slow movements that feel unnatural or disorienting; maintain a consistent, engaging pace.
Do not use extreme wide-angle distortion that makes rooms look unnatural or fish-eyed.
Do not introduce external elements such as people, text overlays, music, or distracting clutter.
Avoid excessive camera height changes or extreme angles that disorient the viewer.
Do not over-saturate colors, apply overly stylized filters, or use artificial/dark lighting.
Do not linger too long on any single feature or area; maintain continuous motion.
No rotation or warping of the image.
